steps.title: Identify the sex-based prediction
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that immature males should direct a higher proportion of peering at immigrants than females do, because males (unlike females) will disperse to new regions and must adapt to them.

steps.title: Match data showing the sex difference
steps.content: Choice C directly demonstrates this sex difference: females (individuals 1 and 6) directed a lower proportion at immigrants than males (individuals 15 and 16). Choices A, B, and D either ignore sex differences, measure total counts instead of proportions, or fail to show the predicted pattern. This matches choice C — C is correct.
